summary: Changes from April 1 2026 LIVE: Several financial and regulatory changes will come into force across banking, fuel, income tax and travel from 1 April. These financial changes will have a direct impact on the day-to-day lives of Indian citizens. Follow for LIVE updates.

title: Can you use two health insurance policies for one hospital bill? Here's what experts say
summary: Many individuals hold multiple health insurance policies, such as a corporate cover and a personal plan. Claiming from both policies is permitted. Here's what policyholders must know.&nbsp;

title: What are balanced advantage funds? 5 reasons why they can help you navigate market volatility
summary: Balanced Advantage Funds can help investors manage market volatility through dynamic asset allocation between equity and debt, offering stability, tax efficiency, and disciplined investing for long term wealth creation.

title: How becoming a loan guarantor can hurt your retirement income and credit score
summary: Pensioners should be mindful when considering becoming a loan guarantor, as a borrower’s default could impact their retirement savings, credit score, and overall financial stability, potentially leading to legal obligations and added financial pressure on a limited income.

title: Is India really one of the cheapest places to retire? ₹1 cr, ₹2 cr or ₹10 cr - How much is enough?
summary: Most global retirement models assume that individuals retire around the age of 60 and need to fund 20–25 years of expenses. That assumption is quietly becoming outdated.

title: Petrol, diesel price build-up: How much tax do you pay on one litre of petrol and diesel? Here's a breakdown
summary: Despite a cut in special additional excise duty on fuel, petrol and diesel prices will not decrease at retail level, as the government absorbs costs to support oil marketing companies amid high international prices. The duty on petrol was reduced from ₹13 to ₹3 per litre and diesel to nil.

title: No more easy rate cuts: What borrowers should expect next
summary: With easy rate cuts on hold, borrowers should expect stable EMIs. The focus should be on affordable loan choices, disciplined repayment, and careful planning, as higher inflation and global uncertainties may push borrowing costs higher in the future.

title: Got a banking complaint? Follow RBI’s 3-step process before approaching Ombudsman
summary: The RBI complaint process guides customers in resolving banking grievances by first approaching regulated entities, obtaining an acknowledgement, and escalating unresolved issues to the RBI Ombudsman for resolution.

title: 10 key financial changes from 1 April 2026 that will directly impact your finances
summary: Financial and regulatory changes in April 2026 include new tax rules, higher rebates, updated TDS forms, increased banking charges, changes in fuel prices and new travel refund rules. Here are 10 financial changes to look out for.
